---
title: "Module 2 Slides — Data Splitting & Preprocessing"
subtitle: "A map before we open the codebook"
date: today
---

## About These Slides

This slide deck gives you a **big-picture overview** of Module 2 before you
work through the full codebook. Think of it as the map, not the territory —
27 slides covering the five stops of the module at a conceptual level, with
just enough code to orient you before you see the full implementation.

Work through these slides **after** the Video Quiz and **before** the
Codebook. They take about 15–20 minutes to read through at your own pace.

---

## What the Slides Cover

The deck is organized around five stops:

**Stop 1 — The Dataset**

- The synthetic direct-mail campaign and its variables
- Why `income` is right-skewed and what that means for modeling
- The log-normal distribution and the geometric standard deviation

**Stop 2 — The Split**

- The golden rule: fit on training data, apply to test data
- Why evaluating on training data is optimistic (data leakage)
- Stratified splitting with `initial_split()` and `strata = responded`

**Stop 3 — The Recipe**

- The cooking metaphor: `recipe()`, `step_*()`, `prep()`, `bake()`
- All six steps in the pipeline and the reason each one exists
- Why `step_impute_median()` beats mean for skewed data
- Why `step_log(..., offset = 1)` needs the offset

**Stop 4 — The Workflow**

- Bundling recipe + model with `workflow()` so nothing gets forgotten
- Regularization at a glance — Ridge, Lasso, Elastic Net
- Why `penalty = 0.01` and `mixture = 1` gives you a Lasso

**Stop 5 — Inspecting the Recipe**

- Using `tidy()` to read what `prep()` learned
- The "what you'll actually type today" summary
- Where this pattern reappears in later modules

---

## Open the Slides

::: {.callout-tip}
The slides open in a **new tab** as a full-screen presentation. Use the
arrow keys or click to advance. Press **F** for full screen, **S** for
speaker notes, and **Esc** to exit full screen.
:::

```{=html}
<a href="w02_ds_preprocessing_slides.html"
   target="_blank"
   rel="noopener noreferrer"
   style="
     display: inline-block;
     margin-top: 0.5rem;
     padding: 0.65rem 1.4rem;
     background-color: #2780e3;
     color: white;
     font-weight: 600;
     font-size: 1rem;
     border-radius: 6px;
     text-decoration: none;
     transition: background-color 0.2s ease;
   "
   onmouseover="this.style.backgroundColor='#1a5fb4'"
   onmouseout="this.style.backgroundColor='#2780e3'"
>
  ▶ Open Slide Deck in New Tab
</a>
```

---

## Key R Functions Introduced in the Slides

| Function | Package | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `initial_split()` | `rsample` | Split data into train/test |
| `training()`, `testing()` | `rsample` | Extract each partition |
| `recipe()` | `recipes` | Declare a preprocessing plan |
| `step_impute_median()` | `recipes` | Fill missing values with median |
| `step_log()` | `recipes` | Log-transform numeric variables |
| `step_normalize()` | `recipes` | Center and scale to mean=0, sd=1 |
| `step_dummy()` | `recipes` | Encode categorical variables as 0/1 |
| `step_zv()` | `recipes` | Remove zero-variance predictors |
| `update_role()` | `recipes` | Mark ID columns as non-predictors |
| `prep()` | `recipes` | Learn transformations from training data |
| `bake()` | `recipes` | Apply learned transformations to any data |
| `workflow()` | `workflows` | Bundle recipe + model spec |
| `fit()` | `workflows` | Train the full pipeline |
| `tidy()` | `broom` | Inspect what a prepped recipe learned |
